scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: every path in Docker Hub's published OpenAPI spec (35 paths, fetched directly from docs.docker.com/reference/api/hub/latest.yaml) is enumerated below. This bundle's spec.json is deliberately credential-free (`{"mode":"none"}`, matching the legacy connector, which never implemented any Docker Hub username/password or PAT auth) — every endpoint requiring an authenticated session (personal/org access tokens, org settings/groups/members/invites, audit logs, SCIM provisioning) is out of scope by design: enabling any of them would first require adding a `POST /v2/users/login` (or PAT bearer) auth exchange to spec.json/streams.json, a capability-surface change beyond a read-stream/write-action expansion, and is left for a future wave if authenticated Docker Hub access becomes a requirement. Practical additions in this pass: `repository_detail` and `tag_detail` single-object streams (previously only the list variants were covered), reusing the already-configured docker_username/repository config.

excluded.reason: current-generation equivalent of the already-covered legacy-parity GET /v2/repositories/{namespace}/ list this bundle's repositories stream reads (identical page/page_size/results envelope) — kept on the legacy path for exact legacy-behavior parity, not re-implemented under the new path
HEAD existence-check only; no response body to stream

current-generation equivalent of the already-covered legacy-parity GET /v2/repositories/{namespace}/{repository}/tags this bundle's tags stream reads (identical page/page_size/results envelope) — kept on the legacy path for exact legacy-behavior parity
HEAD existence-check only; no response body to stream
HEAD existence-check only; no response body to stream
org-admin immutable-tag policy mutation; requires an authenticated session this credential-free public bundle has no auth path for
org-admin immutable-tag policy verification; requires an authenticated session this credential-free public bundle has no auth path for
repository creation requires an authenticated session; legacy never implemented repository create/update and this bundle's spec.json declares no credential to authenticate the request
assigns a Team/group to a repository; org-admin operation requiring an authenticated session not available to this credential-free bundle
destructive repository deletion; not modeled by legacy and requires an authenticated session this bundle has no credential for
username+password session-token exchange; this bundle's spec.json declares no password field and the public registry read surface needs no auth, so no login flow is wired
second-factor login continuation of the excluded /v2/users/login session exchange
scoped registry bearer-token exchange (docker login/pull credential flow); not a data-read endpoint and out of scope for this credential-free public bundle
